items.title: OpenAI shipped its first inference chip
items.oneliner: OpenAI says Jalapeno went from concept to factory-ready in nine months, pushing model economics deeper into custom silicon.

items.title: Gemini Flash now uses computers
items.oneliner: Google added computer use to Gemini 3.5 Flash, making browser and desktop agents a product feature, not a demo.

items.title: Qwen open-sourced an agent world model
items.oneliner: Qwen-AgentWorld simulates seven environments, giving open agents a cheaper training path than burning real browser sessions.

items.title: Anthropic accused Alibaba of model extraction
items.oneliner: Anthropic says Alibaba illicitly extracted Claude capabilities, signaling that model distillation fights are becoming geopolitical and legal.

items.title: Johnson and Johnson exposed employee data
items.oneliner: Two J&J web app flaws exposed data on about 14,600 people, another reminder that basic auth failures still dominate breaches.

items.title: LastPass reported another breach
items.oneliner: LastPass disclosed yet another breach, further weakening the case for trusting centralized password vaults with your blast radius.

items.title: AI spam is flooding open source
items.oneliner: OpenClaw jumped from 2 PRs a week to 3,400, proving AI agents can DDoS maintainer attention.

items.title: MDN launched an MCP server
items.oneliner: Mozilla's MDN MCP server gives coding agents live docs and browser data, which should cut hallucinated web API advice.

items.title: Qualcomm is acquiring Modular
items.oneliner: Qualcomm buying Modular is a serious bet that the AI stack's next margin pool sits in inference compilers.

items.title: Nvidia pushed hotter liquid cooling
items.oneliner: Nvidia says 45C liquid cooling can cut data center water use near zero, making AI infrastructure less thermally absurd.
